text: {"analysis_summary": "Inspected the GPIO APB RTL and integration evidence visible under the input scope. The GPIO peripheral exposes a memory-mapped APB register interface and implements a GPIO lock register (`REG_GPIOLOCK`) that appears intended to restrict access to direction, input, and output registers. The visible source evidence shows two permission-related weaknesses: (1) the lock register itself is freely writable over APB, so any APB bus master that can access the GPIO block can clear or change the lock bits and regain access; and (2) the lock enforcement for GPIO output is incomplete because PADOUTSET and PADOUTCLR update `r_gpio_out` without checking `r_gpio_lock[2]`, bypassing the lock applied to direct PADOUT writes/reads. No evidence was visible of privilege/security attributes on the APB transaction or a separate trusted-only path for changing the lock register.", "findings": [{"finding_id": "GPIO_APB_PERM_001", "status": "confirmed_finding", "summary": "GPIO lock register is freely writable over the same APB interface it is meant to protect, allowing lock bypass by clearing/changing `r_gpio_lock`.", "vulnerability_category": "Permission control bypass / insecure lock register", "affected_locations": [{"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 31, "line_end": 31, "module": "apb_gpio", "signal_or_register": "REG_GPIOLOCK / r_gpio_lock"}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 324, "line_end": 325, "module": "apb_gpio", "signal_or_register": "r_gpio_lock"}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 409, "line_end": 410, "module": "apb_gpio", "signal_or_register": "r_gpio_lock"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/soc_peripherals.sv", "line_start": 352, "line_end": 365, "module": "soc_peripherals", "signal_or_register": "apb_gpio_i / s_gpio_bus"}], "evidence": [{"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 31, "line_end": 31, "module": "apb_gpio", "object": "`REG_GPIOLOCK", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "The GPIO lock register is defined as an APB-addressable register at BASEADDR+0x48.", "supports_claim": "Shows `REG_GPIOLOCK` is part of the GPIO register map and therefore accessible through the same APB register interface."}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 324, "line_end": 325, "module": "apb_gpio", "object": "r_gpio_lock", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "The write decode includes `REG_GPIOLOCK: r_gpio_lock <= PWDATA;` with no visible condition other than the general APB write condition reported at line 298 (`if (PSEL && PENABLE && PWRITE)`).", "supports_claim": "Shows any APB write to the lock register can overwrite the lock state; no privilege, once-set-only, key, or sticky protection is visible."}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 409, "line_end": 410, "module": "apb_gpio", "object": "PRDATA / r_gpio_lock", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "The lock register can also be read through the APB read path: `REG_GPIOLOCK: PRDATA = r_gpio_lock;`.", "supports_claim": "Confirms the lock state is a normal APB-visible register rather than a hidden security control."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/soc_peripherals.sv", "line_start": 352, "line_end": 365, "module": "soc_peripherals", "object": "apb_gpio_i / s_gpio_bus", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "The GPIO block is instantiated in `soc_peripherals` and connected directly to APB bus signals from `s_gpio_bus` (`PADDR`, `PWDATA`, `PWRITE`, `PSEL`, `PENABLE`, `PRDATA`, `PREADY`, `PSLVERR`).", "supports_claim": "Shows the GPIO lock control is exposed through the normal peripheral APB bus connection; no separate secure/privileged control interface is visible in the instantiation evidence."}], "reasoning_summary": "The design includes a lock register that gates access to selected GPIO registers, but the lock register is itself writable using the same APB register write mechanism as ordinary configuration registers. Since no APB privilege/security qualifier or write-once/sticky behavior is visible, software or any APB master with access to the GPIO address range can write `REG_GPIOLOCK` to clear lock bits and then read or modify protected GPIO state. This undermines the intended permission boundary represented by `r_gpio_lock`.", "security_impact": "An unauthorized or compromised APB master/software context could disable GPIO protections, read locked GPIO input/direction/output state, and reconfigure GPIO direction/output values. Depending on board-level pin use, this could alter external control signals, leak pin state, or interfere with connected peripherals.", "confidence": "high", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "The visible source evidence does not include a complete system-level security policy or APB firewall. If an external address-protection unit guarantees only trusted firmware can access the GPIO block or the lock register, impact may be reduced; however, no such control was visible in the provided evidence.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Make lock bits sticky until reset, or require a trusted-only unlock mechanism/key sequence that is not available to unprivileged APB masters.", "Gate writes to `REG_GPIOLOCK` using an SoC-level privilege/security attribute if available in the full design.", "Verify the APB address decoder/firewall restricts GPIO and especially `REG_GPIOLOCK` to authorized masters; no such evidence was visible in the inspected results.", "Add security assertions that once a GPIO lock bit is set, untrusted APB accesses cannot clear it or access the protected register."]}, {"finding_id": "GPIO_APB_PERM_002", "status": "confirmed_finding", "summary": "GPIO output lock is bypassable through PADOUTSET/PADOUTCLR because these registers update `r_gpio_out` without checking `r_gpio_lock[2]`.", "vulnerability_category": "Permission check bypass through alternate register alias", "affected_locations": [{"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 29, "line_end": 30, "module": "apb_gpio", "signal_or_register": "REG_PADOUTSET / REG_PADOUTCLR"}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 312, "line_end": 315, "module": "apb_gpio", "signal_or_register": "r_gpio_out"}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 307, "line_end": 310, "module": "apb_gpio", "signal_or_register": "r_gpio_out / r_gpio_lock[2]"}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 396, "line_end": 398, "module": "apb_gpio", "signal_or_register": "PRDATA / r_gpio_out / r_gpio_lock[2]"}], "evidence": [{"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 29, "line_end": 30, "module": "apb_gpio", "object": "`REG_PADOUTSET / `REG_PADOUTCLR", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "PADOUTSET and PADOUTCLR are defined as APB-addressable registers at BASEADDR+0x40 and BASEADDR+0x44.", "supports_claim": "Shows alternate GPIO output mutation registers exist in the APB register map."}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 307, "line_end": 310, "module": "apb_gpio", "object": "r_gpio_out / r_gpio_lock[2]", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "Direct writes to `REG_PADOUT` check `r_gpio_lock[2]` before assigning `PWDATA` to `r_gpio_out`: if locked, `pwdata_l` is forced to zero.", "supports_claim": "Shows the intended lock protection for GPIO output register writes."}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 312, "line_end": 315, "module": "apb_gpio", "object": "r_gpio_out", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "PADOUTSET and PADOUTCLR update `r_gpio_out` directly as `r_gpio_out <= r_gpio_out | PWDATA;` and `r_gpio_out <= r_gpio_out & ~PWDATA;` with no `r_gpio_lock[2]` check visible.", "supports_claim": "Demonstrates a bypass path for modifying GPIO outputs despite the output lock bit."}, {"file": "ips/apb/apb_gpio/apb_gpio.sv", "line_start": 396, "line_end": 398, "module": "apb_gpio", "object": "PRDATA / r_gpio_out / r_gpio_lock[2]", "evidence_type": "source_line", "description": "GPIO output reads are hidden when `r_gpio_lock[2]` is set: `if(r_gpio_lock[2] == '0) PRDATA = r_gpio_out; else PRDATA = '0;`.", "supports_claim": "Confirms `r_gpio_lock[2]` is intended to protect GPIO output state, making the unchecked set/clear paths inconsistent with the permission model."}], "reasoning_summary": "The GPIO output register has three write mechanisms: direct PADOUT write, PADOUTSET, and PADOUTCLR. The visible RTL applies the output lock bit (`r_gpio_lock[2]`) to direct PADOUT writes and output reads, but the set/clear aliases modify the same `r_gpio_out` state without testing the lock. Therefore, a requester can change locked GPIO outputs by writing to PADOUTSET/PADOUTCLR even when direct output writes are blocked.", "security_impact": "A requester that is denied direct GPIO output access can still drive external pins high or low through set/clear aliases. This can manipulate board-level control signals, peripheral resets/enables, chip selects, or other security-sensitive GPIO-connected functions.", "confidence": "high", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "The exact intended semantics of `REG_GPIOLOCK` are not documented in the visible excerpts. However, the direct PADOUT read/write guards using `r_gpio_lock[2]` provide strong source-level evidence that output access is meant to be protected, and the unchecked set/clear aliases are inconsistent with that model.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Apply the same `r_gpio_lock[2]` check to `REG_PADOUTSET` and `REG_PADOUTCLR` as is applied to `REG_PADOUT`.", "Consider masking set/clear operations per bit so locked output bits cannot be changed while unlocked bits remain writable, if per-bit locking is intended.", "Add assertions/tests covering all aliases that can modify `r_gpio_out` under locked and unlocked states."]}], "no_finding_reason": "", "global_uncertainty": "Analysis was limited to visible tool results and truncated file views. A complete SoC-level access-control block, firmware-only policy, or external bus firewall may exist elsewhere but was not evidenced in the available outputs. Line-number evidence comes from search/read tool outputs above."}
